 {"id":"caam-g2yz.2","title":"Profile Tags for Categorization","description":"## Overview
Add a tagging system for categorizing profiles into groups.

## ⚠️ Priority Note: P3 (Backlog)
This feature adds significant complexity while favorites already provide profile organization. Consider this a "nice to have" after core features are solid.

**Alternative:** Could the favorites system be extended instead? E.g., "favorites groups" or "favorite sets"?

## Problem Statement
As profile counts grow, users need ways to categorize and filter profiles beyond simple listing. Tags enable organizational views by project, team, environment, client, etc.

## Key Distinction from Favorites
- **Favorites:** Ordered priority list (1st favorite, 2nd favorite, etc.)
- **Tags:** Unordered categories (work, personal, project-x, testing)

However, the overlap is significant. Users with 5-10 profiles may not need both systems.

## Design Decisions

### Tag Format
**Decision:** Simple string tags (not key:value pairs)
**Constraints:**
- Lowercase, alphanumeric + hyphens only
- Max 32 characters per tag
- Max 10 tags per profile

### Storage
```go
type Profile struct {
    Tags []string \`json:"tags,omitempty"\`
}
```

## Implementation Tasks
1. Add Tags field to Profile struct
2. Create `caam tag add/remove/list` commands (or `caam profile tag ...`)
3. Add `--tag` filter to `caam ls`
4. TUI integration

## Recommendation
Defer this until users explicitly request it. The Description field (caam-g2yz.1) may be sufficient for most organization needs.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-20T04:33:29.432417046Z","updated_at":"2025-12-20T17:54:31.966502595Z","closed_at":"2025-12-20T17:54:31.966502595Z","close_reason":"Implemented profile tags: Tags field in Profile struct, tag commands (add/remove/list/clear/all), --tag filter for ls","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-g2yz.2","depends_on_id":"caam-g2yz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-20T04:33:29.433694318Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"caam-g4sl","title":"Unit tests for internal/prediction engine","description":"# Task: Unit Tests for internal/prediction Engine

## Overview
Comprehensive unit tests for the depletion prediction engine, burn rate calculation, and alert generation.

## Test Categories

### 1. Burn Rate Calculation Tests
```go
func TestBurnRateCalculation(t *testing.T) {
    tests := []struct {
        name           string
        entries        []*logs.LogEntry
        window         time.Duration
        wantTokens     float64
        wantConfidence float64
    }{
        {
            name: "steady burn rate",
            entries: generateSteadyUsage(1000, 10, time.Hour), // 1000 tokens each, 10 entries over 1 hour
            window: time.Hour,
            wantTokens: 10000.0 / 60, // ~167 tokens/min
            wantConfidence: 0.9,       // High confidence (steady)
        },
        {
            name: "variable burn rate",
            entries: generateVariableUsage(...),
            wantConfidence: 0.5, // Lower confidence (variable)
        },
        {
            name: "no recent data",
            entries: nil,
            wantConfidence: 0.0,
        },
    }
    
    for _, tc := range tests {
        t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
            t.Logf("[TEST] Input: %d entries over %v", len(tc.entries), tc.window)
            
            rate := prediction.CalculateBurnRate(tc.entries, tc.window)
            
            t.Logf("[TEST] Result: %.2f tokens/min, confidence=%.2f", 
                rate.TokensPerMinute, rate.Confidence)
            
            assertApprox(t, tc.wantTokens, rate.TokensPerMinute, 0.1)
            assertApprox(t, tc.wantConfidence, rate.Confidence, 0.1)
        })
    }
}
```

### 2. Depletion Prediction Tests
- Predict depletion with known burn rate
- Predict with zero burn rate (infinite time)
- Predict when already at 100%
- Predict with window reset before depletion

### 3. Warning Level Tests
```go
func TestWarningLevels(t *testing.T) {
    tests := []struct {
        timeUntil   time.Duration
        wantWarning WarningLevel
    }{
        {2 * time.Hour, WarningNone},
        {25 * time.Minute, WarningApproaching},
        {5 * time.Minute, WarningImminent},
        {0, WarningImminent},
    }
    
    for _, tc := range tests {
        t.Run(tc.timeUntil.String(), func(t *testing.T) {
            pred := &Prediction{
                PredictedTime: time.Now().Add(tc.timeUntil),
            }
            t.Logf("[TEST] Time until depletion: %v", tc.timeUntil)
            t.Logf("[TEST] Expected warning: %v", tc.wantWarning)
            
            warning := pred.GetWarningLevel()
            t.Logf("[TEST] Actual warning: %v", warning)
            
            assert.Equal(t, tc.wantWarning, warning)
        })
    }
}
```

### 4. Alert Generation Tests
- Generate alert when approaching limit
- No duplicate alerts for same condition
- Clear alert when condition resolves
- Multiple profiles with different alerts

### 5. Confidence Calculation Tests
- High sample size = high confidence
- Low variance = high confidence
- Recent data = higher weight
- Stale data = lower confidence

## Logging Requirements
- Log all input parameters
- Log intermediate calculations
- Log final predictions with confidence intervals
- Log warning level determinations

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Burn rate calculation tested with various patterns
- [ ] Depletion prediction accuracy verified
- [ ] Warning levels trigger at correct thresholds
- [ ] Alert generation logic tested
- [ ] Confidence scoring tested
- [ ] Edge cases handled (no data, stale data)

## Files to Create
- internal/prediction/engine_test.go
- internal/prediction/burnrate_test.go
- internal/prediction/alerts_test.go
- internal/prediction/testdata/usage_patterns.go

 {"id":"caam-g8mv","title":"Task: Reassess Claude token expiry/refresh features","description":"Goal
- Validate whether Claude token expiry parsing and refresh are accurate; disable if not.

Details
- Audit health.ParseClaudeExpiry + refresh.RefreshClaudeToken assumptions against current .credentials.json fields.
- If expiry/refresh cannot be verified, hard-disable refresh for Claude with a clear message.
- Ensure health scoring and status output do not imply valid expiry when data is opaque.
- Update docs/CLI to avoid implying proactive refresh works for Claude if it does not.
- Emit structured logs when refresh is skipped/disabled (reason, provider, file paths).

Tests
- Unit tests for expiry parsing with modern Claude credentials (opaque tokens).
- Unit tests for refresh-disabled path (explicit warning + no file writes).
- E2E test that simulates Claude auth files and ensures status output is honest.

Success
- Refresh logic is either correct and tested, or explicitly disabled with user-facing rationale.

 {"id":"caam-g9dl","title":"Task: caam setup distributed (auto-config)","description":"Goal
- Provide a single command that configures coordinator(s) and local agent across machines.

Details
- Deploy binary to remotes via SSH/SFTP if needed with version checks and checksum verification.
- Generate config files for coordinator + agent.
- Install/enable systemd user units on Linux and optional launchd on macOS.
- Support dry-run and explicit remote list overrides.
- Require explicit confirmation before any remote changes unless --yes is provided.
- Surface missing dependency checks (wezterm CLI, tailscale, systemd) before attempting changes.

Success
- One command brings the distributed auth system online end-to-end with safe defaults.

 {"id":"caam-rsf","title":"[EPIC] Comprehensive Test Coverage Initiative","description":"# Test Coverage Initiative

## Overview
This epic tracks the comprehensive effort to achieve full unit test coverage (without mocks/fakes) and create a detailed e2e integration test suite with granular logging.

## Current State (as of 2025-12-19)
- **Overall Coverage**: ~70% of packages have test coverage
- **Critical Gaps**:
  - sync/ package: 30% coverage (pool, connpool, discovery untested)
  - bundle/ package: 50% coverage (encrypt.go, validate.go untested)  
  - tui/ package: 35% coverage (many UI components untested)
  - cmd/caam/cmd/: 63% coverage (8 commands untested)
- **Test Approach**: Real implementations preferred over mocks

## Goals
1. 100% unit test coverage for all exported functions
2. Real implementation tests (no mocks/fakes/stubs)
3. Comprehensive e2e integration test suite
4. Detailed, structured logging in all tests using testutil.Harness pattern

## Child Epics
- Critical Security Tests (bundle encryption/validation)
- Sync Infrastructure Tests (pool, connpool, discovery)
- Refresh Module Tests
- TUI Component Tests
- CLI Command Tests
- E2E Integration Test Suite

## Success Criteria
- All packages at 95%+ test coverage
- Zero use of mock objects
- E2E tests cover all major workflows
- Test logs provide clear debugging context","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-19T23:57:23.070720117Z","updated_at":"2025-12-20T03:05:51.260842236Z","closed_at":"2025-12-20T03:05:51.260842236Z","close_reason":"All 7 child EPICs completed: caam-nra (Security Tests), caam-krd (Sync Tests), caam-gux (E2E Tests), caam-ctw (Utility Tests), caam-41a (CLI Tests), caam-a0p (TUI Tests), caam-dxf (Refresh Tests)","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-rsf","depends_on_id":"caam-41a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-20T00:09:48.129883101Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-rsf","depends_on_id":"caam-a0p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-20T00:09:53.235504467Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-rsf","depends_on_id":"caam-ctw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-20T00:09:43.021291995Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-rsf","depends_on_id":"caam-dxf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-20T00:09:58.342306997Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-rsf","depends_on_id":"caam-gux","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-20T00:09:37.914988756Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-rsf","depends_on_id":"caam-krd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-20T00:09:32.808764134Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-rsf","depends_on_id":"caam-nra","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-20T00:09:27.701378647Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"caam-s2u0","title":"TUI: Wire delete action to vault.Delete() - already has confirmation","description":"Wire handleDeleteProfile() confirmation to call vault.Delete().

CURRENT STATE: handleDeleteProfile() (model.go:814-826) sets confirmDelete state. The confirmation handler at line 1146 just says 'not yet implemented'.

IMPLEMENTATION:
1. In confirmation handler for confirmDelete, call vault.Delete(provider, profileName)
2. Handle errors gracefully
3. Refresh profile list after deletion
4. Show success message with profile name

EDGE CASES:
- Profile doesn't exist (already deleted)
- Profile is active (should we warn? CLI doesn't)
- Permission denied on vault directory

 {"id":"caam-sogx","title":"Epic: Fast profile selection UX","description":"Purpose
- Provide a zero-friction way to select and activate profiles when limits hit, without memorizing full emails.

Background
- Users often have many profiles; typing full email slows them down.
- A picker should reduce context switches and be reliable over SSH.

Scope
- Fuzzy picker (fzf) with CLI fallback when fzf or TTY is unavailable.
- Alias support and fuzzy matching integration.
- Documentation and shell alias examples.

Success Criteria
- One command selects and activates a profile in under a second.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T16:27:02.138878958Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T16:27:02.138878958Z","compaction_level":0}
